source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In a gravity-free space, there is a Cartesian coordinate system xOy. In the first and fourth quadrants, there is a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to the plane of the paper and directed inward, with a magnetic induction strength of $B = 5 \times 10^{-2} \, \text{T}$. A lightweight, insulating, smooth thin rod lies along the y-axis, with its lower end at point O. The length of the rod is $h = 0.1 \, \text{m}$. At the top of the rod, there is a negatively charged small ball that can slide freely along the rod. The mass of the ball is $m = 1 \times 10^{-5} \, \text{kg}$, and its charge is $q = 1 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{C}$. Below the x-axis, there is a barrier PQ (sufficiently long) parallel to the y-axis, with point P located on the x-axis at coordinates $x_p = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{5} \, \text{m}$. Assume all friction is negligible.

1. When a thin rod moves at a constant velocity $v_0 = 4 \, \text{m/s}$ along the positive x-axis under the action of a horizontal external force $F$, the motion characteristics of the small ball before it detaches from the rod and the time it takes for the ball to detach from the rod.
2. In problem 1, the work $W$ done by the horizontal external force $F$.
3. To make the small ball hit the left side of the baffle, the range of the uniform velocity of the thin rod moving to the right.

image_dependency: The problem relies on a spatial setup in a coordinate plane: magnetic-field regions by quadrant, a rod on the y-axis, and a baffle at a specified x-position, which is diagram-friendly and image-supported.
asset_registry_record shows one valid image and registry_passed=true.
However, the text itself provides many geometric details and coordinates, so the image support appears important but not strictly irreplaceable.
multi_step: The question has three numbered subproblems: detachment motion/time, work by external force, and the velocity range for hitting the left side of the baffle.

Solving requires combining multiple physics components rather than a single lookup: magnetic force effects, constrained motion along the rod, detachment condition, energy/work analysis, and range/case analysis.
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true is consistent with substantial reasoning depth.
verifiability: answer_text gives explicit final outputs: a time, a work value, and an inequality range.
The outputs are concise physical quantities with units or a bounded interval, which are amenable to downstream checking.
answer_type=set and the three-part question structure make expected answer slots clear, though ordered alignment must be preserved.

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和图片都存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空。
初步评分较高：multi_step_score=0.94、verifiability_score=0.86、image_dependency_score=0.74，说明题目具有较强推理价值且可校验性较好。
虽然存在 mixed_numeric_and_inequality_answer_format 和 multi_part_answer_alignment 风险，但这些更像后续解析与对齐问题，不构成核心语义缺失。
题目文本长度充足且图文共同支撑语义，具备进入候选池的条件。

In a gravity-free space, there is a Cartesian coordinate system xOy. In the first and fourth quadrants, there is a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to the plane of the paper and directed inward, with a magnetic induction strength of $B = 5 \times 10^{-2}\,\text{T}$. A lightweight, insulating, smooth thin rod lies along the y-axis, with its lower end at point O. The length of the rod is $h = 0.1\,\text{m}$. At the top of the rod, there is a negatively charged small ball that can slide freely along the rod. The mass of the ball is $m = 1 \times 10^{-5}\,\text{kg}$, and its charge is $q = 1 \times 10^{-3}\,\text{C}$. Below the x-axis, there is a barrier PQ (sufficiently long) parallel to the y-axis, with point P located on the x-axis at coordinates $x_p = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{5}\,\text{m}$. Assume all friction is negligible.

1. When the thin rod moves at a constant velocity $v_0 = 4\,\text{m/s}$ along the positive x-axis under the action of a horizontal external force $F$, determine the motion characteristics of the small ball before it detaches from the rod and the time it takes for the ball to detach from the rod.
2. In problem 1, determine the work $W$ done by the horizontal external force $F$.
3. To make the small ball hit the left side of the baffle, determine the range of the uniform velocity of the thin rod moving to the right.

agent_assessment.rationale: The text fully specifies the physics setup, parameters, and requested outputs, while the diagram clearly supports the spatial geometry: axes, rod on the y-axis, magnetic-field region, and vertical barrier at point P. The expected answers are present and align with the multi-part question.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample is complete and solvable, with clear answer signal and usable rewrite output, but the alignment record is marked risky with moderate consistency and two conflicts. It remains semantically usable, so review is preferred over rejection.

conflict_pairs.detail: 文本中的视觉锚点明显多于可识别视觉区域。
题目依赖图像，但题干中视觉锚点表达较弱。
